evals.prompt: Write Terraform to provision an AWS VPC with public and private subnets, NAT gateway, an ECS Fargate cluster, an RDS PostgreSQL instance, and an ALB. Use modules for each component.
evals.expected_output: Modular Terraform config with VPC, subnets, NAT, ECS, RDS, ALB, proper networking

assertions.name: modular_structure
assertions.description: Separate modules for VPC, ECS, RDS, ALB

assertions.name: vpc_networking
assertions.description: Public and private subnets with NAT gateway

assertions.name: rds_in_private
assertions.description: RDS in private subnet, not publicly accessible

assertions.name: remote_state
assertions.description: Remote state backend configured (S3 + DynamoDB)

assertions.name: variables_used
assertions.description: No hardcoded values — all configurable via variables

evals.prompt: Create Terraform for a multi-environment setup (dev, staging, prod). Each environment should have the same infrastructure but different sizing. Dev gets small instances, prod gets large. Use workspaces or separate tfvars.
evals.expected_output: Multi-environment Terraform with per-environment variables and consistent infrastructure

assertions.name: per_env_config
assertions.description: Separate tfvars or workspace-based config per environment

assertions.name: size_differences
assertions.description: Instance sizes vary by environment (dev=small, prod=large)

assertions.name: shared_modules
assertions.description: Same modules used across all environments

assertions.name: state_isolation
assertions.description: Each environment has isolated state

assertions.name: naming_convention
assertions.description: Resources named with environment prefix

evals.prompt: Write Terraform to set up an S3 bucket for static website hosting with CloudFront distribution, ACM certificate for HTTPS, and Route53 DNS record. The domain is example.com.
evals.expected_output: Terraform for S3 static site + CloudFront + ACM + Route53

assertions.name: s3_website
assertions.description: S3 bucket configured for static website hosting

assertions.name: cloudfront_dist
assertions.description: CloudFront distribution with S3 origin

assertions.name: acm_certificate
assertions.description: ACM certificate for HTTPS

assertions.name: route53_record
assertions.description: Route53 A record aliased to CloudFront

assertions.name: bucket_policy
assertions.description: S3 bucket policy allowing CloudFront access (OAI or OAC)

evals.prompt: We need to import an existing AWS RDS instance into Terraform management. The instance ID is myapp-prod-db. Write the Terraform resource block and the import command.
evals.expected_output: Terraform resource matching existing RDS config, import command, and state verification

assertions.name: resource_block
assertions.description: aws_db_instance resource matching existing configuration

assertions.name: import_command
assertions.description: terraform import command with correct resource address and ID

assertions.name: plan_after_import
assertions.description: Instructs to run terraform plan to verify no changes

assertions.name: state_backup
assertions.description: Recommends backing up state before import

assertions.name: lifecycle_prevent
assertions.description: Suggests lifecycle prevent_destroy for production databases

evals.prompt: Write a Terraform module for creating an ECS Fargate service. Inputs: service name, container image, port, CPU, memory, desired count, VPC ID, subnet IDs, ALB target group ARN. Outputs: service ARN and task definition ARN.
evals.expected_output: Reusable Terraform module with variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf for ECS Fargate

assertions.name: module_structure
assertions.description: variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf in module directory

assertions.name: all_inputs
assertions.description: Variables for all specified inputs with types and descriptions

assertions.name: ecs_resources
assertions.description: ECS task definition, service, and IAM role created

assertions.name: outputs_defined
assertions.description: Service ARN and task definition ARN as outputs

assertions.name: security_group
assertions.description: Security group created or accepted as input for the service
